purpose: Concept-level structural awareness for agent. Not for final structural design.
safetyNote: Never provide final beam/column/rebar/foundation design as certified output. Mark all structure outputs as conceptual and require structural engineer verification.

planningRules: Keep columns aligned in a logical grid.
Avoid random column positions blocking parking/living/bedroom usability.
Wet areas and shafts should not cut major structural members without engineering review.
Large spans need structural review before finalization.
Avoid excessive wall removal in renovation without structural review.
Openings in load-bearing walls require lintel/structural checks.
Material flow, clear height, bay spacing, loading access, and crane/equipment needs affect structure.
Bracing and service routing should be coordinated.
Coordinate architectural grid, structure grid, shafts, HVAC, fire, and ceiling depth.

structuralCoordinationChecks: Column grid should not conflict with doors, parking bays, stair flights, toilets, or major furniture.
Stair opening should be structurally feasible.
Double-height spaces and large cantilevers need explicit structural warning.
Renovation involving wall removal needs structural check.
